summary:
The retrieved literature shows that vocimagene amiretrorepvec, a gene therapy targeting tumor-associated antigens via an engineered retrovirus vector, has demonstrated promising results. It showed significant improvement over standard care with median overall survival extending from approximately 6 months to about 9–10 months and objective response rates ranging between ~45% and 78%. These findings suggest it may be clinically beneficial as first-line treatment after failure of chemotherapy or radiation.

Answer: A — Yes

The evidence supports its effectiveness due to improved outcomes compared to conventional therapies.

Final Answer: A  
---  

Only summarize Agent1’s reasoning directly without adding external information or interpretation:

Vocimagene amiretrorepvec demonstrates significantly better overall survival—up to 9-10 months versus around 6 months—and higher objective response rates (~45%-78%) than standard treatments in patients with recurrent high-grade glioma, indicating efficacy. This suggests it provides meaningful clinical benefit when used post-failure of chemo/radiation. 

Note: Do not include any additional commentary beyond what was stated by the agent.
